Flavor-Text: Its jaws are strong enough to crush rocks but so heavy that it can’t get up if it flips over. Sandile seize those moments as their chance.

Name: Trapinch
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: Its jaws are strong enough to crush rocks but so heavy that it can’t get up if it flips over. Sandile seize those moments as their chance.

Name: Vibrava
Ability-Effect: Whenever your opponent plays a Supporter card from their hand, prevent all effects of that card done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It vibrates its wings to generate ultrasonic waves, causing its prey to faint. Then it buries the prey alive in the sand to preserve it.

Name: Flygon-GX
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, all of your Pokémon take 30 less damage from your opponent’s attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

This attack’s damage isn’t affected by any effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Anorith
Flavor-Text: When restored Anorith are released into the ocean, they don’t thrive, because the water composition has changed since their era.

Name: Armaldo
Effects: This attack does 50 more damage for each Unidentified Fossil card in your discard pile.
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
Flavor-Text: It lived on land and went out into the sea to hunt for prey. Its sharp claws were its greatest weapon.

Name: Groudon
Effects: Attach up to 2 [F] Energy cards from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon.
This Pokémon can’t use Trembling Ground during your next turn.
Flavor-Text: Said to have expanded the lands by evaporating water with raging heat. It battled titanically with Kyogre.

Name: Drilbur
Effects: Shuffle a card from your discard pile into your deck.
Flavor-Text: It makes its way swiftly through the soil by putting both claws together and rotating at high speed.

Name: Excadrill
Effects: If there are 3 or fewer cards in your deck, this attack does 150 more damage.
Discard the top 4 cards of your deck.
Flavor-Text: It can help in tunnel construction. Its drill has evolved into steel strong enough to bore through iron plates.

Name: Palpitoad
Effects: This attack does 10 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: When they vibrate the bumps on their heads, they can make waves in water or earthquake-like vibrations on land.

Name: Seismitoad
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for a card, shuffle your deck, then put that card on top of it.
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each of your Benched Pokémon that has any damage counters on it.
Flavor-Text: It increases the power of its punches by vibrating the bumps on its fists. It can turn a boulder to rubble with one punch.

Name: Throh
Effects: If your Benched Pokémon have any damage counters on them, this attack does 90 more damage.
Flavor-Text: When it tightens its belt, it becomes stronger. Wild Throh use vines to weave their own belts.

Name: Pancham
Flavor-Text: It follows Pangoro around like a henchman. When Pancham makes a big mistake, its leaf gets taken away.

Name: Pangoro
Flavor-Text: This rowdy Pokémon boasts great physical strength. Many Trainers are also smitten by its lively character.

Name: Crabrawler
Flavor-Text: Its hard pincers are well suited to both offense and defense. Fights between two Crabrawler are like boxing matches.

Name: Crabominable
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: If this Pokémon has any [W] Energy attached to it, this attack does 80 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It stores coldness in its pincers and pummels its foes. It can even smash thick walls of ice to bits!

Name: Rockruff
Effects: Your opponent switches their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: As they develop, their disposition grows more violent and aggressive. Many Trainers find them too much to handle and abandon them.

Name: Lycanroc
Ability-Effect: If your opponent has any Pokémon-GX or Pokémon-EX in play, this Pokémon’s attacks cost [C][C][C] less.
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon has any Special Energy attached to it, this attack does 70 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It has no problem ignoring orders it doesn’t like. It doesn’t seem to mind getting hurt at all—as long as it can finish off its opponent.

Name: Passimian
Effects: Draw 2 cards.
Flavor-Text: They use their saliva to stick leaves to their shoulders. You can tell what troop they belong to from the position of the leaves.

Name: Sandygast
Effects: Choose a random card from your opponent’s hand. Your opponent reveals that card and shuffles it into their deck.
Flavor-Text: It likes the shovel on its head, so Sandygast will get serious and fight any children who come to take it back.

Name: Palossand
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 20 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
This attack does 20 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Each of its grains of sand has its own will. Palossand eats small Pokémon and siphons away their vital essence while they’re still alive.

Name: Alolan Meowth
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It’s impulsive, selfish, and fickle. It’s very popular with some Trainers who like giving it the attention it needs.

Name: Alolan Persian-GX
Ability-Effect: Prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon by your opponent’s TAG TEAM Pokémon and Ultra Beasts, and by your opponent’s Pokémon that have any Special Energy attached to them.
Effects: This attack does 120 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This damage isn’t affected by Weakness, Resistance, or any other effects on that Pokémon. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Alolan Grimer
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: There are a hundred or so of them living in Alola’s waste-disposal site. They’re all hard workers who eat a lot of trash.

Name: Alolan Muk
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned, Confused, and Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: There are over a hundred kinds of poison inside its body. Chemical reactions between different poisons are the source of its vitality.

Name: Carvanha
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: They’re such cowards that they won’t hunt alone. When five or so of them get together, they suddenly turn ferocious!

Name: Absol
Effects: Discard a Special Energy from 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Discard a card from your hand. If you can’t discard a card, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: The only thing unlucky about Absol is its appearance. It protects fields and warns people of disaster, so one ought to be grateful for it.

Name: Pawniard
Effects: Your opponent reveals their hand. Discard an Item card you find there.
Flavor-Text: After shredding its prey, it sharpens its blades on a stone by the river. Each Pawniard has its own favorite sharpening stone.

Name: Bisharp
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
This Pokémon can’t use Slashing Strike during your next turn.
Flavor-Text: No matter how strong the Bisharp, it’s said that if the blade on its head is chipped, it will retire from its position as the boss.

Name: Guzzlord
Effects: Discard the top card of your opponent’s deck.
If your opponent’s Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from this attack, take 1 more Prize card.
Flavor-Text: Although it’s alien to this world and a danger here, it’s apparently a common organism in the world where it normally lives.

Name: Alolan Sandshrew
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: After fleeing a volcanic eruption, it ended up moving to an area of snowy mountains. Its ice shell is as hard as steel.

Name: Alolan Sandslash
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks during your opponent’s next turn.
If this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool card attached to it, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It runs across snow-covered plains at high speeds. It developed thick, sharp claws to plow through the snow.

Name: Steelix
Effects: Discard any number of Pokémon with a Retreat Cost of exactly 4 from your hand. This attack does 50 damage for each card you discarded in this way.
Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 100 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Tempered underground under high pressure and heat, its body is harder than any metal.

Name: Mawile
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 TAG TEAM cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: A cunning and terrifying Pokémon, its cuteness makes opponents let down their guard—and then it swallows them whole with its huge jaws.

Name: Probopass
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flip 3 coins. This attack does 40 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Although it can control its units known as Mini-Noses, they sometimes get lost and don’t come back.

Name: Solgaleo

Effects: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

Name: Alolan Ninetales
Effects: This attack does 10 damage for each Pokémon Tool card in your discard pile.

Name: Azurill
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may flip a coin. If heads, attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to your Active Pokémon. If you use this Ability, your turn ends.
Flavor-Text: A Pokémon that lives by water. It moves quickly on land by bouncing on its big tail.

Name: Cottonee
Effects: This attack does 20 damage for each of your Pokémon, except [*] (Prism Star) Pokémon, in the Lost Zone.
Flavor-Text: To protect itself, it shoots cotton from its body. When it gets wet in the rain, its cotton grows moist and heavy, and it can’t move as well.

Name: Whimsicott
Effects: Put a card from your hand in the Lost Zone. If you do, draw 3 cards.
This attack does 20 damage for each of your Pokémon, except [*] (Prism Star) Pokémon, in the Lost Zone.
Flavor-Text: This nuisance sneaks into people’s homes, where it hides important things and scatters cotton all over the place.

Name: Flabébé
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 [Y] Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: It’s not safe without the power of a flower, but it will keep traveling around until it finds one with the color and shape it wants.

Name: Flabébé
Effects: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It’s not safe without the power of a flower, but it will keep traveling around until it finds one with the color and shape it wants.

Name: Floette
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may choose 2 random cards from your opponent’s hand. Your opponent reveals those cards and shuffles them into their deck.
Flavor-Text: It raises flowers and uses them as weapons. The more gorgeous the blossom, the more power it contains.

Name: Florges
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may choose 2 random cards from your opponent’s hand. Your opponent reveals those cards and shuffles them into their deck.
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 60 damage for each heads. This Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: It controls the flowers it grows. The petal blizzards that Florges triggers are overwhelming in their beauty and power.

Name: Swirlix
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 10 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: To entangle its opponents in battle, it extrudes white threads as sweet and sticky as cotton candy.

Name: Slurpuff
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon isn’t Confused, this attack does nothing.

Name: Jangmo-o
Effects: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

Name: Jangmo-o
Flavor-Text: It smacks the scales on its head against rocks or against the ground to frighten its opponents. It can also contact its friends with these noises.

Flavor-Text: It makes noise by clanging its scales together. When the rhythm has reached its peak, Hakamo-o attacks.

Effects: Attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
You may discard a Pokémon Tool card from this Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 90 more damage.
Flavor-Text: When it howls after finishing off its prey, the metallic sounds of its celebrating comrades can be heard from all around.

Name: Ultra Necrozma
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon can’t attack unless your opponent has 2 or fewer Prize cards remaining.

Name: Aipom

Flavor-Text: As it did more and more with its tail, its hands became clumsy. It makes its nest high in the treetops.

Name: Ambipom
Effects: Draw 2 cards.
Discard up to 2 cards from your hand. This attack does 60 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

Name: Teddiursa
Flavor-Text: If it finds honey, its crescent mark glows. It always licks its paws because they are soaked with honey.

Name: Ursaring

Name: Stoutland

Name: Rufflet

Name: Braviary

Name: Heliolisk
Effects: If your opponent has any [L] Pokémon in play, this attack does 90 more damage.

Name: Stufful
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It boasts power enough to split large trees in half. The organ on its rear releases an odor that it uses to communicate with others of its kind.
